case_title: ROBIN MATTHEWS VS. KNOWLEDGE NATION, INC. et al

docket_entries.description: 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF MATTHEWS, ROBIN AS TO DEFENDANT KNOWLEDGE NATION, INC. ENTELLECT LIMITED DOES 1 TO 50 INCLUSIVE
POS OF OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S PETITION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION; DECLARATION OF ROBIN MATTHEWS FILED BY PLAINTIFF MATTHEWS, ROBIN
NOTICE OF JOINDER AND JOINDER TO PETITION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION FILED BY DEFENDANT ENTELLECT LIMITED HEARING SET FOR MAY-23-2013 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT ENTELLECT LIMITED'S JOINDER TO PETITION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. THE SCOPE OF THE DISPUTES IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ARE ALL WITHIN THE SCOPE OF THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE. (PAR. 15 OF THE EXECUTIVE EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT DATED AS OF APRIL 1, 2012) ("PAR. 15"). THE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET HIS BURDEN OF PROOF TO DEMONSTRATE A LEGALLY JUSTIFIABLE BASIS FOR NOT ENFORCING THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET HIS BURDEN OF PROOF THAT THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE WAS A CONDITION OF HIS EMPLOYMENT, SO THE COSTS OF ARBITRATION ARE TO BE ALLOCATED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE RULES OF THE AMERICAN ARBITRATION ASSOCIATION AND PAR. 15. SEE ARMENDARIZ V. FOUNDATION HEALTH CARE (2000) 24 CAL.4TH 83. PURSUANT TO PAR. 15, EACH PETITIONER IS ENTITLED TO ITS ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS INCURRED IN THIS ACTION. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

THE SCOPE OF THE DISPUTES IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ARE ALL WITHIN THE SCOPE OF THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE. (PAR. 15 OF THE EXECUTIVE EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT DATED AS OF APRIL 1, 2012) ("PAR. 15"). Ntc Of Hearing On Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration
Off Calendar May-23-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KNOWLEDGE NATION, INC.'s Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration is granted. The scope of the disputes in the First Amended Complaint are all within the scope of the arbitration clause. (Par. 15 of the Executive Employment Agreement dated as of April 1, 2012) ("Par. 15"). The Plaintiff has not met his burden of proof to demonstrate a legally justifiable basis for not enforcing the arbitration clause. Plaintiff has not met his burden of proof that the arbitration clause was a condition of his employment, so the costs of arbitration are to be allocated in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association and Par. 15. See Armendariz v. Foundation Health Care (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83. Pursuant to Par. 15, each Petitioner is entitled to its attorney's fees and costs incurred in this action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Joinder And Joinder To Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration
Off Calendar May-23-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ENTELLECT LIMITED's Joinder To Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration is granted. The scope of the disputes in the First Amended Complaint are all within the scope of the arbitration clause. (Par. 15 of the Executive Employment Agreement dated as of April 1, 2012) ("Par. 15"). The Plaintiff has not met his burden of proof to demonstrate a legally justifiable basis for not enforcing the arbitration clause. Plaintiff has not met his burden of proof that the arbitration clause was a condition of his employment, so the costs of arbitration are to be allocated in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association and Par. 15. See Armendariz v. Foundation Health Care (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83. Pursuant to Par. 15, each Petitioner is entitled to its attorney's fees and costs incurred in this action. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
